note:
{
  "experiment_overview": "The experiment investigates simultaneous multi-surface anodizations and stair-like reverse biases detachment of anodic aluminum oxides (AAOs) in sulfuric and oxalic acid electrolytes. The goal is to fabricate additional AAOs using a sequential process involving substrate preparation and electrochemical etching.",
  "procedures_observed": [
    "A person in a lab coat and gloves prepares a sample by placing it into a container filled with a solution. They then use a machine to measure the sample's properties.",
    "The person adjusts settings on a machine, likely for electrochemical etching, and places the sample back into the solution.",
    "The person uses a device to measure the current and voltage during the etching process."
  ],
  "materials_and_subjects": [
    "Aluminum substrates",
    "Sulfuric and oxalic acid electrolytes",
    "Electrochemical etching machine"
  ],
  "tools_and_setup": [
    "Machine for measuring sample properties",
    "Machine for adjusting etching parameters",
    "Device for measuring current and voltage"
  ],
  "quantitative_observations": [
    "Voltage settings: 21.04 V, 22.04 V, 23.04 V, 24.05 V",
    "Current measurements during etching process"
  ],
  "key_transitions": [
    "Preparation of the sample",
    "Adjustment of etching parameters",
    "Measurement of current and voltage during etching"
  ],
  "outcomes_or_indicators": [
    "Fabrication of additional AAOs",
    "Changes in sample properties over time"
  ],
  "anything_unusual_or_notable": [
    "Sequential process of substrate preparation and electrochemical etching",
    "Use of different electrolytes (sulfuric and oxalic acid)"
  ]
}
